pattern: Tracking, for every reachable (position-parity, alternating-sum) pair, the full set of exact products a subsequence could have built so far -- plus a lightweight flag for 'some subsequence already went over the limit but could still be rescued by a later zero' -- turns an exponential search into a bounded sweep, since one zero anywhere always collapses a product straight to zero.
hints: A chosen element's sign in the alternating sum depends only on whether it lands at an even or odd position within the subsequence so far, not on its original index -- so track state as (parity of picks made, running alternating sum).

For each such state, you need the full set of distinct exact products achievable (not just the best one), because a smaller product now can survive a later multiplication that would push a larger one over the limit -- but any product that already exceeds the limit can only ever become useful again by later being multiplied by a literal 0.

So keep, per (parity, running sum), the set of achievable products that are still within the limit, plus a separate flag meaning 'some subsequence reaches this state already over the limit'; extend both when a new element is skipped or taken, and read off the answer as the largest tracked product whose running sum equals k after all elements are processed.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, the full array is the best subsequence
statement example, the target alternating sum is unreachable
statement example, the largest-product subsequence exceeds the limit so a smaller one wins
a single zero element, its own alternating sum and product are both zero
a single element at the maximum value with a generous limit
a target sum that is simply out of range for any subsequence of this array
every nonzero-product subsequence reaching the target sum exceeds the limit, but a subsequence containing a zero also reaches the target sum, so the answer is exactly zero rather than -1
a negative target alternating sum achievable only through a specific odd/even placement of a repeated value
several ones mixed in, which shift the alternating sum without touching the product at all

stress.description: random values across the full 0-12 range near the maximum subsequence length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(150, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(-min(1800, 12 * n), min(1800, 12 * n))
    limit = rng.randint(1, 5000)
    return [nums, k, limit]

complexity.time: O(n * range(alternating sum) * average reachable products)
